Question_EN: What is the income tax rate on salary in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: Income tax uses a graduated system: 7% for N300k–N600k; 11% for N600k–N1.1m.
Keywords: PIT, salary, rate, graduated

Question_EN: Who is exempt from paying personal income tax?
Answer_EN: Individuals earning below N300,000 per year are exempt from paying PIT.
Keywords: PIT, exempt, N300k, threshold

Question_EN: How is tax deducted from a resident individual's employment income?
Answer_EN: It is typically deducted at source via the Pay As You Earn (PAYE) system by the employer.
Keywords: PAYE, employer, deduct, employment

Question_EN: Are benefits-in-kind taxable for employees?
Answer_EN: Yes, benefits-in-kind are considered part of employment income and are subject to tax.
Keywords: benefits, in-kind, taxable, employment

Question_EN: What is presumptive taxation for individuals?
Answer_EN: It is a simplified tax regime for individuals whose actual income cannot be easily determined.
Keywords: presumptive, simplified, income

Question_EN: What is the standard rate of tax for companies?
Answer_EN: Companies are taxed based on size; small companies at 0%, others at 30%.
Keywords: CIT, company, 0%, 30%, rate

Question_EN: How are the total profits of a company ascertained?
Answer_EN: Total profits are determined by aggregating assessable profits from all sources for the year.
Keywords: CIT, profits, assessable, aggregate

Question_EN: Are export processing and free trade zone entities taxable?
Answer_EN: Such entities have specific exemptions and rules under the specialized trade provisions.
Keywords: free trade zone, export, exempt

Question_EN: How is the lottery and gaming trade taxed?
Answer_EN: These are categorised as specialized trades with specific assessment criteria under the Act.
Keywords: lottery, gaming, specialised, tax

Question_EN: What is a 'chargeable gain' for tax purposes?
Answer_EN: A gain that occurs upon the disposal of chargeable assets as defined by the Act.
Keywords: CGT, chargeable gain, disposal, assets

Question_EN: Does compulsory acquisition of land trigger CGT?
Answer_EN: Specific provisions apply to the disposal of land under compulsory acquisition.
Keywords: CGT, land, compulsory acquisition

Question_EN: What is the Hydrocarbon Tax?
Answer_EN: A tax charged on profits from certain upstream petroleum operations in Nigeria.
Keywords: hydrocarbon, petroleum, upstream, tax

Question_EN: Are incentives available for the utilisation of associated gas?
Answer_EN: Yes, the Act provides specific incentives to encourage the utilisation of associated gas.
Keywords: gas, incentive, associated gas

Question_EN: Are passenger road-transport services exempt from VAT?
Answer_EN: Shared passenger road-transport services are generally exempt from VAT.
Keywords: VAT, transport, exempt, bus

Question_EN: Is agricultural equipment subject to VAT?
Answer_EN: Purchase or lease of tractors and equipment for agricultural purposes is exempt.
Keywords: VAT, agriculture, tractor, exempt

Question_EN: What expenses are allowed to be deducted from business profits?
Answer_EN: Expenses must be wholly and exclusively incurred for the production of income to be deductible.
Keywords: deduction, allowable, business, expenses

Question_EN: Are penalties paid to the government tax-deductible?
Answer_EN: No, any expenditure incurred as a penalty (e.g., gas flare fees) is not a deductible expense.
Keywords: penalty, non-deductible, gas flare

Question_EN: Can I deduct the cost of capital assets from my daily business income?
Answer_EN: Capital expenditure is generally not allowed as a deduction; capital allowances are claimed instead.
Keywords: capital, allowance, deduction, asset

Question_EN: What is the basis for computing assessable profits for a business?
Answer_EN: Assessable profits are generally based on the profits of the accounting period preceding the year of assessment.
Keywords: assessable profits, accounting period, basis

Question_EN: What happens to tax when a company permanently stops its trade?
Answer_EN: Specific cessation rules apply for computing assessable profits in the year a business shuts down.
Keywords: cessation, trade, shutdown, assessable

Question_EN: How is tax calculated for a new business starting for the first time?
Answer_EN: New businesses follow specific commencement rules to determine their assessable profits for their first few years.
Keywords: new business, commencement, first year

Question_EN: What is the Development Levy under the 2025 Act?
Answer_EN: It is a levy charged to support national infrastructure, security, and education funds.
Keywords: development levy, infrastructure, security

Question_EN: How is the Development Levy money distributed?
Answer_EN: Split between Nigeria Police (15%), Nigerian Education Loan (15%), and other security/tech funds.
Keywords: development levy, police, education, distribution

Question_EN: What is the Defence and Security Infrastructure Fund?
Answer_EN: A fund established by the Act to implement national security schemes and develop the defence industry.
Keywords: defence fund, security, infrastructure

Question_EN: Is milk subject to VAT in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: No, milk (fresh or processed) is exempt from VAT.
Keywords: VAT, milk, exempt, food

Question_EN: Are medical services for animals taxable?
Answer_EN: Medical services for both humans and animals rendered by a qualified practitioner are VAT exempt.
Keywords: VAT, veterinary, medical, exempt

Question_EN: Are farm tractors subject to VAT?
Answer_EN: The purchase, hire, or lease of tractors and agricultural equipment is VAT exempt.
Keywords: VAT, tractor, farm, exempt, agriculture

Question_EN: What is the basis period for income tax on petroleum operations?
Answer_EN: The basis period is generally the accounting period of the company engaged in petroleum operations.
Keywords: petroleum, basis period, income tax

Question_EN: How is the 'Adjusted Profit' of a petroleum company calculated?
Answer_EN: It is the profit of the period after specific deductions allowed under the Act, such as those in Section 97.
Keywords: adjusted profit, petroleum, deductions

Question_EN: What defines 'Petroleum' under Chapter Three of the Act?
Answer_EN: It includes hydrocarbons in their natural state, such as crude oil, natural gas, and condensates.
Keywords: petroleum, definition, crude oil, gas

Question_EN: Are royalties for natural gas and gas liquids different from crude oil?
Answer_EN: Yes, the Act specifies that royalties for natural gas follow a distinct set of rules.
Keywords: royalty, gas, crude oil, liquids

Question_EN: What happens if a company sells chargeable oil below the fiscal price?
Answer_EN: Additional tax may be payable if the sale price is lower than the established fiscal oil price.
Keywords: fiscal price, chargeable oil, additional tax

Question_EN: What is 'Cost Oil' in a Production Sharing Contract?
Answer_EN: The portion of available crude oil used to reimburse the contractor for operating and capital costs.
Keywords: PSC, cost oil, contractor, recover

Question_EN: How is 'Tax Oil' defined in the 2025 Act?
Answer_EN: The portion of available crude oil used by the holder to settle the contractor's income tax liabilities.
Keywords: PSC, tax oil, contractor, liability

Question_EN: Who determines quality and gravity differentials for Nigerian crude?
Answer_EN: Differentials are based on the established fiscal oil price for Nigerian crude of comparable quality.
Keywords: crude, gravity, fiscal price, differentials

Question_EN: Can a tax officer act without the standard seven-day notice?
Answer_EN: Yes, an officer not below the rank of Chief Inspector may act without notice in specific urgent cases.
Keywords: tax officer, notice, 7 days, urgent

Question_EN: What is the minimum notice period for document requests from the tax service?
Answer_EN: Generally at least seven days from the date of service.
Keywords: notice, 7 days, documents, FIRS

Question_EN: What is 'Qualifying Expenditure' for capital allowance purposes?
Answer_EN: Capital expenditure on assets like plant, machinery, or buildings used for business.
Keywords: qualifying expenditure, capital allowance, plant
Source: FIRS Tax Law Compendium, Fourth Schedule

Question_EN: How is the 'Residue' of qualifying expenditure calculated?
Answer_EN: It is the total expenditure minus the total allowances previously granted for that asset.
Keywords: residue, qualifying expenditure, allowance
Source: FIRS Tax Law Compendium, Fourth Schedule

Question_EN: Does a new lease on the same asset count as separate expenditure?
Answer_EN: If a new lease is granted to the same lessee on termination, it is treated as a continuation of the first lease.
Keywords: lease, continuation, allowance, asset
Source: FIRS Tax Law Compendium, Fourth Schedule

Question_EN: Are electronic transfers below N10,000 subject to tax?
Answer_EN: No, electronic transfers or receipts below N10,000 are exempt from duty.
Keywords: stamp duty, N10k, exempt, transfer

Question_EN: Is the transfer of shares in Nigerian companies exempt from stamp duty?
Answer_EN: Yes, all documents relating to transfer of stocks and shares are exempt from stamp duties.
Keywords: stamp duty, shares, exempt, stocks

Question_EN: Are salary payments exempt from the Electronic Money Transfer Levy?
Answer_EN: Yes, transfers made for salary payments are specifically exempt from the levy.
Keywords: EMTL, salary, exempt, transfer

Question_EN: Do intra-bank self-transfers attract electronic transfer charges?
Answer_EN: No, transfers between a person's own accounts within the same bank are exempt.
Keywords: intra-bank, self-transfer, exempt, EMTL

Question_EN: Are instruments executed by co-operative societies subject to stamp duty?
Answer_EN: No, any instrument executed by a registered co-operative society is exempt.
Keywords: co-operative, stamp duty, exempt

Question_EN: What is the new Capital Gains Tax (CGT) rate for companies?
Answer_EN: The standard CGT rate for companies is aligned with CIT at 30% under the 2025 Act.
Keywords: CGT, companies, 30%, rate

Question_EN: Are digital assets like cryptocurrencies subject to Capital Gains Tax?
Answer_EN: Yes, chargeable assets now explicitly include digital assets, cryptocurrencies, and digital tokens.
Keywords: CGT, crypto, digital assets, NFT

Question_EN: When is the sale of shares in a Nigerian company exempt from CGT?
Answer_EN: Exempt if proceeds are less than N150m and the chargeable gain does not exceed N10m in 12 months.
Keywords: CGT, shares, exempt, N150m, N10m

Question_EN: Are gains from government bonds taxable?
Answer_EN: No, income generated from government bonds is listed as exempt income.
Keywords: CGT, bonds, government, exempt

Question_EN: What is the 'Electronic Fiscal System' (EFS)?
Answer_EN: A digital system mandated for recording and reporting all taxable supplies to ensure accuracy.
Keywords: EFS, electronic, fiscal, digital, system

Question_EN: When must a business file its monthly return for chargeable services?
Answer_EN: Returns must generally be submitted by the 21st day of the following month.
Keywords: monthly return, 21st day, filing

Question_EN: What is the deadline for reporting foreign exchange transactions?
Answer_EN: Foreign exchange transactions must be reported within seven days.
Keywords: forex, FX, 7 days, report

Question_EN: What is 'Presumptive Tax' under the new law?
Answer_EN: A simplified tax assessment used when a taxpayer's actual income cannot be determined due to lack of records.
Keywords: presumptive tax, records, simplified

Question_EN: Who is the 'Tax Ombud' established by the 2025 reforms?
Answer_EN: The Tax Ombud is an office created to handle and resolve taxpayer grievances and disputes.
Keywords: tax ombud, grievances, disputes, taxpayer

Question_EN: What is the difference between VAT 'Exempt' and 'Zero-Rated' (0%) supplies?
Answer_EN: Exempt means no VAT charged and no input tax can be claimed. Zero-rated means 0% charged but input tax can be claimed.
Keywords: VAT, exempt, zero-rated, input tax, difference

Question_EN: Are basic food items like bread and rice subject to VAT?
Answer_EN: No, basic food items (bread, cereals, vegetables) are Zero-Rated (0%) to keep them affordable.
Keywords: VAT, food, bread, rice, zero-rated, exempt

Question_EN: Does a student pay VAT on school fees (tuition)?
Answer_EN: No, tuition for nursery, primary, secondary, and tertiary education is Zero-Rated (0%).
Keywords: VAT, school fees, tuition, education, zero-rated

Question_EN: Is electricity supply subject to VAT?
Answer_EN: Electricity across the entire value chain (generation to consumer) is now Zero-Rated (0%).
Keywords: VAT, electricity, zero-rated, DISCO, GENCO

Question_EN: Do I pay VAT on medical services or medicines?
Answer_EN: Medical services and pharmaceutical products are Zero-Rated (0%); cosmetology is excluded.
Keywords: VAT, medical, medicine, pharmaceutical, zero-rated

Question_EN: Are baby products like diapers and milk taxed?
Answer_EN: No, baby products for children aged 0–3 years are VAT Exempt under the 2025 Act.
Keywords: VAT, baby, diapers, exempt, children

Question_EN: What is the VAT treatment for public transport?
Answer_EN: Shared passenger road transport is VAT Exempt; ride-hailing (Uber/Bolt) is not exempt.
Keywords: VAT, transport, danfo, keke, exempt, uber

Question_EN: Do exporters pay VAT on goods sent out of Nigeria?
Answer_EN: Exported goods and services (except oil and gas) are Zero-Rated (0%) to encourage exports.
Keywords: VAT, export, zero-rated, goods, international

Question_EN: What is the 'Development Levy' introduced in 2025?
Answer_EN: A single 4% levy on the assessable profits of medium and large companies, replacing several old taxes.
Keywords: development levy, 4%, TET, police levy

Question_EN: Do small businesses pay the 4% Development Levy?
Answer_EN: No, small companies (turnover ≤N100m and fixed assets ≤N250m) are exempt.
Keywords: development levy, small business, exempt

Question_EN: Are digital products or electronic goods subject to excise duty?
Answer_EN: Yes, the 2025 Act expands excise duties to cover certain digital products and locally manufactured electronics.
Keywords: excise, digital, electronics, duty

Question_EN: Who is responsible for collecting Customs and Excise duties?
Answer_EN: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) remains the sole authority for assessing and collecting these duties.
Keywords: customs, NCS, excise, collection

Question_EN: What is the penalty for making a false declaration to Customs?
Answer_EN: A false declaration can lead to a N5 million fine or imprisonment, plus seizure of the goods.
Keywords: customs, false declaration, penalty, N5m

Question_EN: What is 'VAT Fiscalisation'?
Answer_EN: A mandatory digital system where businesses must report every taxable sale to the tax authority in real-time.
Keywords: VAT, fiscalisation, digital, real-time, EFS

Question_EN: Is there a tax on fossil fuels in the 2025 Act?
Answer_EN: Yes, a 5% Fossil Fuel Surcharge; excludes household kerosene, LPG (cooking gas), and CNG.
Keywords: fossil fuel, surcharge, 5%, kerosene, LPG, CNG

Question_EN: What is Withholding Tax (WHT) in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: WHT is an advance payment of income tax, deducted at the source of a transaction and remitted to FIRS.
Keywords: WHT, withholding tax, advance, source, FIRS

Question_EN: What is the WHT rate for consultancy or professional services?
Answer_EN: 5% for individuals and 10% for corporate entities.
Keywords: WHT, consultancy, professional, 5%, 10%

Question_EN: Does Withholding Tax apply to dividends?
Answer_EN: Yes, dividends are subject to WHT at 10%; may vary for non-residents under tax treaties.
Keywords: WHT, dividend, 10%, non-resident, treaty

Question_EN: Is WHT a final tax for everyone?
Answer_EN: No, WHT is usually a credit against the total tax liability at year-end, except for certain income types.
Keywords: WHT, final tax, credit, annual

Question_EN: What is a 'WHT Credit Note'?
Answer_EN: A document proving that tax was deducted from your payment; used to reduce your total annual tax bill.
Keywords: WHT, credit note, FIRS, reduce tax

Question_EN: What is Stamp Duty?
Answer_EN: Tax on written or electronic instruments (documents) such as agreements, contracts, and receipts.
Keywords: stamp duty, document, agreement, contract

Question_EN: Does every bank transfer attract the Electronic Money Transfer Levy?
Answer_EN: The EMT Levy of N50 applies to electronic transfers of N10,000 or more into a bank account.
Keywords: EMTL, bank transfer, N50, N10k

Question_EN: Are rent agreements subject to Stamp Duty?
Answer_EN: Yes, lease and tenancy agreements must be stamped at rates based on the duration of the lease.
Keywords: stamp duty, rent, lease, agreement

Question_EN: What is the penalty for failing to deduct Withholding Tax?
Answer_EN: Penalty of 10% of the amount not deducted, plus interest at the prevailing CBN rate.
Keywords: WHT, penalty, 10%, CBN rate

Question_EN: When must WHT be remitted to the FIRS?
Answer_EN: WHT must be remitted within 30 days of the date the amount was deducted.
Keywords: WHT, remit, 30 days, FIRS, deadline

Question_EN: Do I need to pay Stamp Duty on a gift of land?
Answer_EN: Yes, instruments conveying property as a gift are subject to stamp duty based on property value.
Keywords: stamp duty, gift, land, property

Question_EN: Is there WHT on interest earned from savings?
Answer_EN: Yes, interest earned is generally subject to WHT at 10%, deducted by the bank.
Keywords: WHT, savings, interest, 10%, bank

Question_EN: What happens if a document is not stamped?
Answer_EN: Unstamped documents may not be admissible as evidence in court for civil proceedings.
Keywords: stamp duty, unstamped, court, inadmissible

Question_EN: Are small businesses exempt from deducting WHT?
Answer_EN: Small businesses with turnover below the VAT threshold are often exempt from acting as WHT agents.
Keywords: WHT, small business, exempt, agent

Question_EN: Who pays the Stamp Duty on a contract between two parties?
Answer_EN: Unless agreed otherwise, the person receiving the document or beneficiary of the contract usually pays.
Keywords: stamp duty, who pays, contract, beneficiary

Question_EN: How are companies classified for tax under the 2025 Act?
Answer_EN: Small (turnover ≤N50m), Medium (>N50m to N100m), and Large (>N100m).
Keywords: CIT, company, small, medium, large, turnover

Question_EN: What is the CIT rate for a Small Company?
Answer_EN: Small companies (turnover ≤N50m) are subject to 0% CIT, provided not in professional services.
Keywords: CIT, small company, 0%, exempt

Question_EN: What is the CIT rate for a Medium Company?
Answer_EN: Medium companies with turnover N50m–N100m are taxed at 20%.
Keywords: CIT, medium company, 20%, turnover

Question_EN: What is the CIT rate for a Large Company?
Answer_EN: Large companies with turnover exceeding N100m are taxed at 30%.
Keywords: CIT, large company, 30%, turnover

Question_EN: Do small companies still need to file tax returns?
Answer_EN: Yes, all companies must file annual tax returns regardless of tax size exemption.
Keywords: CIT, small company, file, returns, mandatory

Question_EN: What is the new Development Levy?
Answer_EN: A consolidated 4% levy on assessable profits of medium and large companies, replacing Education Tax.
Keywords: development levy, 4%, TET, education tax

Question_EN: Are small companies required to pay the 4% Development Levy?
Answer_EN: No, small companies (turnover ≤N50m) are exempt from the 4% Development Levy.
Keywords: development levy, small company, exempt

Question_EN: What is the 'Minimum Tax' for companies?
Answer_EN: Companies with no profit pay a minimum tax of 0.5% of gross turnover, unless in first 4 years.
Keywords: minimum tax, 0.5%, turnover, company

Question_EN: Which companies are exempt from Minimum Tax?
Answer_EN: Small companies, those in their first four years, and primary agricultural businesses are exempt.
Keywords: minimum tax, exempt, agriculture, first 4 years

Question_EN: What is the deadline for filing CIT returns?
Answer_EN: Returns must be filed within 6 months after the end of the company's accounting year.
Keywords: CIT, filing, deadline, 6 months

Question_EN: Can a company claim 'Capital Allowance' on all assets?
Answer_EN: Yes, but you must provide proof of VAT payment or import duties to claim allowances on qualifying assets.
Keywords: capital allowance, VAT proof, qualifying assets

Question_EN: What is the tax rate for 'Professional Service' companies?
Answer_EN: Professional service companies (law, accounting) are taxed at 30% regardless of turnover.
Keywords: CIT, professional services, 30%, law, accounting

Question_EN: How is 'Agricultural Business' taxed?
Answer_EN: Primary agricultural businesses enjoy a 5-year tax-free period from the date they commence operations.
Keywords: agriculture, 5-year, tax-free, pioneer

Question_EN: What is the penalty for late filing of CIT?
Answer_EN: Penalty is N50,000 for the first month of default and N25,000 for every subsequent month.
Keywords: CIT, late filing, penalty, N50k, N25k

Question_EN: What is the 'Effective Tax Rate' for MNEs?
Answer_EN: Large MNEs with global turnover above €750m are subject to a 15% global minimum tax.
Keywords: MNE, ETR, 15%, global minimum tax, multinational

Question_EN: Are dividends received by a company taxable?
Answer_EN: Dividends received from a Nigerian company are 'franked investment income' and not subject to further CIT.
Keywords: dividend, franked investment, CIT, exempt

Question_EN: How is the 'Hydrocarbon Tax' calculated for upstream companies?
Answer_EN: Hydrocarbon Tax is levied on profits from crude oil at 30% for onshore/shallow water and 15% for PPLs.
Keywords: hydrocarbon tax, 30%, 15%, onshore, shallow water

Question_EN: What is the total tax rate for deep offshore oil operations?
Answer_EN: Deep offshore operations are exempt from Hydrocarbon Tax but subject to 30% CIT.
Keywords: petroleum, deep offshore, CIT, 30%

Question_EN: Can petroleum companies deduct 'Decommissioning' costs?
Answer_EN: Yes, contributions to a decommissioning fund are tax-deductible if backed by a certificate from the Commission.
Keywords: decommissioning, petroleum, deductible, fund

Question_EN: What is the new Withholding Tax (WHT) rate for residents?
Answer_EN: The WHT rate is generally 5% for most services provided by companies and individuals.
Keywords: WHT, resident, 5%, services

Question_EN: Is WHT a final tax for small businesses?
Answer_EN: For certain small-scale suppliers, the WHT deducted at source may be treated as their final tax liability.
Keywords: WHT, final tax, small business

Question_EN: What is the WHT rate on Dividends and Interest?
Answer_EN: Dividends, Interest, and Royalties paid to residents are subject to 10% Withholding Tax.
Keywords: WHT, dividend, interest, royalty, 10%

Question_EN: How many days does a company have to remit WHT?
Answer_EN: WHT must be remitted to the FIRS within 21 days after the end of the month of deduction.
Keywords: WHT, remit, 21 days, FIRS

Question_EN: What happens if a company fails to deduct WHT?
Answer_EN: Failure to deduct results in a penalty of 10% of the tax due, plus interest at the prevailing CBN rate.
Keywords: WHT, penalty, 10%, CBN, failure

Question_EN: Are 'Signature Bonuses' tax-deductible?
Answer_EN: No, signature bonuses and production bonuses paid to the Government are not tax-deductible.
Keywords: signature bonus, petroleum, non-deductible

Question_EN: Do I need a Tax Identification Number (TIN) for WHT?
Answer_EN: Yes, a valid TIN is required for both payer and payee to ensure the WHT credit is properly assigned.
Keywords: WHT, TIN, credit, payer, payee

Question_EN: Is WHT applied to the purchase of raw materials?
Answer_EN: Generally, direct purchase of goods in the ordinary course of business is exempt from WHT.
Keywords: WHT, raw materials, goods, exempt

Question_EN: What is the tax treatment for 'Associated Gas' profits?
Answer_EN: Profits from associated gas are taxed under CIT at 30%, rather than higher PPT/Hydrocarbon rates.
Keywords: associated gas, CIT, 30%, petroleum

Question_EN: Can a non-resident claim a lower WHT rate?
Answer_EN: Yes, if from a country with a Double Taxation Treaty with Nigeria, rate is capped at 7.5%.
Keywords: WHT, non-resident, treaty, DTT, 7.5%

Question_EN: What is the filing deadline for Petroleum profits?
Answer_EN: Estimated returns within 2 months of start of year; final returns within 5 months of year-end.
Keywords: petroleum, filing, deadline, 2 months, 5 months

Question_EN: How do I use a Withholding Tax Credit Note?
Answer_EN: Use the credit note issued by FIRS to reduce the actual CIT you owe at the end of the year.
Keywords: WHT, credit note, FIRS, CIT offset

Question_EN: What is the Capital Gains Tax (CGT) rate for companies?
Answer_EN: Under the 2025 Act, capital gains for companies are taxed at the standard CIT rate of 30%.
Keywords: CGT, company, 30%, disposal

Question_EN: Are digital assets like Bitcoin subject to Capital Gains Tax?
Answer_EN: Yes, the 2025 Act explicitly includes digital assets, cryptocurrencies, and tokens as chargeable assets.
Keywords: CGT, bitcoin, crypto, digital assets, NFT

Question_EN: Is there a CGT exemption for selling my primary home?
Answer_EN: Yes, an individual is entitled to a one-time lifetime exemption on their principal private residence.
Keywords: CGT, primary home, exemption, lifetime, residence

Question_EN: Do individuals pay a flat 10% for Capital Gains?
Answer_EN: No, the flat 10% is abolished; individuals' gains are now taxed at progressive PIT rates (0–25%).
Keywords: CGT, 10%, abolished, progressive, individual

Question_EN: Who pays the Electronic Money Transfer Levy (EMTL) starting 2026?
Answer_EN: From Jan 1, 2026, the N50 levy on transfers of N10,000 or more must be paid by the Sender.
Keywords: EMTL, sender, N50, N10k, 2026

Question_EN: What is the deadline for stamping a legal document?
Answer_EN: Most legal instruments must be stamped within 30 days of execution to be legally admissible.
Keywords: stamp duty, 30 days, deadline, admissible

Question_EN: What is the stamp duty rate for property leases of 8–21 years?
Answer_EN: Lease agreements for 8–21 years attract an ad valorem stamp duty rate of 3%.
Keywords: stamp duty, lease, 3%, ad valorem, property

Question_EN: Is there an excise duty on sugar-sweetened beverages?
Answer_EN: Yes, a minimum excise duty of N10 per litre on non-alcoholic, carbonated, and sweetened beverages.
Keywords: excise, sugar, beverages, N10, litre

Question_EN: What are 'Health Taxes' in the 2025 Act?
Answer_EN: Excise duties on tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks designed to discourage consumption.
Keywords: health tax, tobacco, alcohol, excise, sugar

Question_EN: Are gains from selling Nigerian shares exempt from tax?
Answer_EN: Gains are exempt if total proceeds are less than N150 million within a 12-month period.
Keywords: CGT, shares, exempt, N150m, 12 months

Question_EN: What is the fixed duty for general contracts?
Answer_EN: For contracts not otherwise specified with a percentage, a fixed stamp duty of N1,000 applies.
Keywords: stamp duty, N1000, contract, fixed

Question_EN: Who is responsible for collecting and remitting Excise Duty?
Answer_EN: The manufacturer or importer of excisable goods is responsible for collecting and remitting to FIRS.
Keywords: excise, manufacturer, importer, remit, FIRS

Question_EN: Can I deduct the cost of improving an asset from the CGT?
Answer_EN: Yes, cost of acquisition and capital expenditure used to improve the asset's value are deductible.
Keywords: CGT, improvement, deduction, acquisition cost

Question_EN: What happens if I use an unstamped document in court?
Answer_EN: An unstamped document is generally not admissible as evidence until the duty and penalties are paid.
Keywords: stamp duty, unstamped, court, civil proceedings

Question_EN: Does the 2025 Act apply excise to telecommunication services?
Answer_EN: The Act provides a framework for excise on specific services; specific rates may be set by the Minister.
Keywords: excise, telecom, internet, services

Question_EN: What is the tax rate for a 'Small Company' in 2025?
Answer_EN: Small companies (annual turnover under N50 million) are subject to 0% Corporate Income Tax.
Keywords: small company, 0%, CIT, turnover N50m

Question_EN: Must a small business with 0% tax still file returns?
Answer_EN: Yes, all companies must file tax returns annually, even if they qualify for the 0% small business rate.
Keywords: small business, file, returns, mandatory

Question_EN: What is the turnover threshold for a Medium Company?
Answer_EN: Medium companies are those with annual turnover between N50 million and N1.5 billion.
Keywords: medium company, turnover, N50m, N1.5bn

Question_EN: What is the penalty for late filing of tax returns?
Answer_EN: N50,000 for the first month of default and N25,000 for each subsequent month.
Keywords: penalty, late filing, N50k, N25k

Question_EN: What happens if I provide false information to the FIRS?
Answer_EN: Providing false or misleading information can lead to a fine of up to N10 million or imprisonment.
Keywords: penalty, false, N10m, imprisonment, FIRS

Question_EN: Is there a penalty for not registering for tax?
Answer_EN: Yes, failure to register for tax within the required time attracts a penalty of N50,000 for the first month.
Keywords: penalty, registration, N50k, TIN

Question_EN: Are religious organizations exempt from all taxes?
Answer_EN: Exempt only on income from non-profit activities; any trading or business income is taxable.
Keywords: religious, exempt, non-profit, trading income

Question_EN: Is my pension income taxable in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: No, any profit or income from a statutory pension scheme or retirement savings account is exempt.
Keywords: pension, exempt, retirement, savings

Question_EN: Do I pay tax on compensation for loss of office?
Answer_EN: Compensation for loss of office is exempt from tax up to N10,000,000.
Keywords: compensation, loss of office, N10m, exempt

Question_EN: Can a small business claim 'Small Business Relief'?
Answer_EN: Yes, small businesses are eligible for various incentives, including simplified accounting and 0% income tax.
Keywords: small business, relief, incentive, 0%

Question_EN: How long should I keep my tax records?
Answer_EN: You are required to keep all tax-related records and documents for a minimum of 6 years.
Keywords: records, 6 years, FIRS, audit

Question_EN: What is the penalty for failing to deduct Withholding Tax?
Answer_EN: Penalty is 10% of the amount not deducted, plus interest at the prevailing CBN rate.
Keywords: WHT, penalty, 10%, CBN, deduction

Question_EN: Are dividends from small companies taxable?
Answer_EN: Dividends paid by a small company are generally exempt from further tax in the hands of the recipient.
Keywords: dividend, small company, exempt, franked

Question_EN: What is the CIT rate for a 'Medium Company'?
Answer_EN: Medium companies (turnover N50m–N1.5bn) are taxed at a reduced rate of 20%.
Keywords: CIT, medium company, 20%

Question_EN: Can I pay my taxes in instalments?
Answer_EN: Yes, taxpayers can apply to the FIRS to pay assessed tax in instalments, subject to approval.
Keywords: instalments, FIRS, approval, payment plan

Question_EN: Will transfers and deposits into my bank account be taxed?
Answer_EN: No. Bank transfers, POS movements, and deposits are not taxable events; only income earned is taxed.
Keywords: bank, POS, transfer, deposit, not taxable

Question_EN: Will I be denied access to my bank account if I don't have a TIN by Jan 1?
Answer_EN: No. You can still access existing accounts; new accounts will require a TIN over time.
Keywords: TIN, bank account, access, January

Question_EN: What are the new progressive tax bands starting in 2026?
Answer_EN: First N800k (0%), next N2.2m (15%), next N9m (18%), next N13m (21%), next N25m (23%), above N50m (25%).
Keywords: PIT, tax bands, progressive, 2026, N800k

Question_EN: Are military salaries taxable under the new law?
Answer_EN: No. Salaries of military officers are now specifically exempt from personal income tax.
Keywords: military, army, salary, exempt, PIT

Question_EN: Do musicians and actors still have tax exemptions on foreign income?
Answer_EN: No. Authors, musicians, and creatives must now pay Nigerian tax on income earned within and outside Nigeria.
Keywords: musician, actor, creative, foreign income, tax

Question_EN: Are loans from lenders like Fairmoney taxable as income?
Answer_EN: No. Loans are not income and are not taxable; the lender's interest income is taxable for them.
Keywords: loan, Fairmoney, not taxable, income

Question_EN: Are disability pensions for injured soldiers taxable?
Answer_EN: No. Disability pensions for members of the armed forces are completely tax-exempt.
Keywords: disability, pension, military, exempt

Question_EN: What is the 'Rent Relief' benefit under the new law?
Answer_EN: From 2026, individuals can claim tax relief of 20% of annual rent, capped at N500,000.
Keywords: rent relief, 20%, N500k, 2026, deduction

Question_EN: Is income from Federal or State Government bonds taxable?
Answer_EN: No. All government bonds are specifically listed as tax-exempt.
Keywords: bonds, government, exempt, investment

Question_EN: Will I pay tax on a big severance package when I leave my job?
Answer_EN: Severance pay of N50 million or less is tax-free; any amount above that is taxed using progressive bands.
Keywords: severance, N50m, tax-free, termination

Question_EN: Will Nigeria tax dividends or rent I earn from properties abroad?
Answer_EN: Dividends and rent earned abroad are exempt if brought into Nigeria through approved banking channels.
Keywords: foreign income, dividend, rent, approved channels

Question_EN: As a remote worker for an international company, do I pay tax in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: Yes, especially if your income is exempt in the company's home country due to a treaty or diplomatic arrangement.
Keywords: remote work, international, tax, Nigeria

Question_EN: How long does it take to get a Tax Clearance Certificate (TCC)?
Answer_EN: A TCC should be issued within two weeks after all taxes for the last three years have been fully paid.
Keywords: TCC, 2 weeks, clearance, FIRS

Question_EN: Does a 'Business Name' pay PIT or CIT?
Answer_EN: If registered as a business name (enterprise), you pay PIT. If registered as a limited liability company, you pay CIT.
Keywords: business name, enterprise, PIT, CIT, LLC

Question_EN: Is there a limit on assets for small company exemptions?
Answer_EN: Yes, a company is generally exempt if it has up to N100 million turnover or N250 million in assets.
Keywords: small company, N100m, N250m, assets, exempt

Question_EN: What is the exact tax for someone earning N100k/month?
Answer_EN: After the N800k exemption and deductions (Pension/NHF/Rent), an N100k earner pays almost zero tax (~N600/month).
Keywords: PIT, N100k, monthly, calculation, N600

Question_EN: Do I pay tax to the FIRS or my State?
Answer_EN: PIT is paid to your State IRS (e.g., KIRS for Kano). FIRS only handles CIT and VAT.
Keywords: FIRS, State IRS, PIT, CIT, VAT, jurisdiction

Question_EN: Does a foreign company need to register in Nigeria to hire me?
Answer_EN: No. Foreign employers of remote workers no longer need to register in Nigeria solely because of remote staff.
Keywords: remote work, foreign employer, registration

Question_EN: If I earn USD/Euro, how do I calculate my tax?
Answer_EN: Convert the foreign currency to Naira using the prevailing CBN rate on the day of the transaction.
Keywords: USD, EUR, CBN rate, convert, Naira, tax

Question_EN: Can I claim rent relief if I live with my parents for free?
Answer_EN: No. Rent relief requires documentary evidence (receipts) of actual rent paid to a landlord.
Keywords: rent relief, evidence, receipt, landlord

Question_EN: Is there VAT on renewable energy like solar panels?
Answer_EN: No. Renewable energy equipment including solar panels and batteries is now VAT-exempt.
Keywords: VAT, solar, renewable energy, exempt, panels

Question_EN: Is public transportation (bus/train) subject to VAT?
Answer_EN: No. Public transportation and related services are specifically exempt from VAT in the 2025 Act.
Keywords: VAT, bus, train, transport, exempt

Question_EN: What is the deadline to file my individual tax return?
Answer_EN: You must file your personal annual tax return by March 31st of every year.
Keywords: PIT, March 31, filing deadline, annual return

Question_EN: What is the 'Tax Incentives Return'?
Answer_EN: A new mandatory filing for businesses enjoying tax holidays to prove they still qualify.
Keywords: incentive, pioneer status, mandatory, filing

Question_EN: How long does the FIRS have to audit my old taxes?
Answer_EN: Generally 6 years. However, if fraud or wilful default is discovered, there is no time limit.
Keywords: audit, 6 years, fraud, FIRS, limitation

Question_EN: What happened to the Tertiary Education Tax (TET)?
Answer_EN: TET, the IT Levy, and the Police Trust Fund Levy have all been merged into the new 4% Development Levy.
Keywords: TET, IT levy, police levy, development levy, merged

Question_EN: Does the 0% tax apply to professional firms (Law/Accounting)?
Answer_EN: No. Small companies providing professional services (Law, Consulting) are excluded from the 0% CIT rate.
Keywords: professional firms, 0%, excluded, law, accounting

Question_EN: What if I sell an asset and use the money to buy another?
Answer_EN: If you reinvest the proceeds into a similar asset within the same year, you may be exempt from CGT.
Keywords: CGT, reinvest, rollover, exempt, same year

Question_EN: Can the tax office enter my house to check my records?

Keywords: tax office, warrant, court order, home, audit

Question_EN: Do I pay VAT on my children's school fees?
Answer_EN: No. Education and educational materials remain strictly VAT-exempt.
Keywords: VAT, school fees, education, children, exempt

Question_EN: What is the penalty for 'Tax Avoidance' schemes?
Answer_EN: You must disclose aggressive tax planning; failure leads to heavy fines and interest.
Keywords: tax avoidance, penalty, fines, disclosure

Question_EN: What is a 'Tax Ombudsman'?
Answer_EN: An independent office where you can complain if a tax official is mistreating you or your business.
Keywords: ombudsman, complaint, tax official, independent

Question_EN: Is there WHT on a small shop's supply?
Answer_EN: No. There is zero WHT on transactions involving small businesses (turnover < N50m).
Keywords: WHT, small shop, small business, zero, exempt

Question_EN: Are 'Benefits-in-Kind' (like a company car) taxed?

Keywords: benefits-in-kind, company car, taxable, employment

Question_EN: Why did VAT increase to 10%?
Answer_EN: To increase domestic revenue; the law balances this by exempting nearly all essential costs for poor households.
Keywords: VAT, 10%, increase, essential, poor, revenue

Question_EN: Do I pay tax on money I win from a lottery or gaming?
Answer_EN: Yes. Winnings from lotteries, betting, and gaming are subject to tax, usually deducted at the source.
Keywords: lottery, gaming, winnings, tax, betting

Question_EN: Can I get a refund if I overpaid my tax?
Answer_EN: Yes. The law mandates that valid tax refunds must be processed within 90 days of the application.
Keywords: refund, overpaid, 90 days, FIRS

Question_EN: Is my interest income from a Savings Account taxed?
Answer_EN: Interest on personal savings up to a certain limit is generally exempt or taxed at a low WHT rate.
Keywords: savings, interest, WHT, exempt, bank

Question_EN: Does a company in a Free Trade Zone pay tax?
Answer_EN: Only on the portion of goods sold inside Nigeria; goods exported 100% are tax-exempt.
Keywords: free trade zone, export, tax-exempt, customs territory

Question_EN: Is the N800,000 threshold monthly or annual?
Answer_EN: It is Annual. Monthly, it means anyone earning roughly N66,667 or less pays zero tax.
Keywords: PIT, N800k, annual, monthly, N66k, threshold

Question_EN: Why is the government reforming the tax system now?
Answer_EN: To create a fair system, reduce the number of taxes, and ensure the rich pay their fair share while protecting the poor.
Keywords: reform, fair, reduce taxes, rich, poor

Question_EN: Will the new laws increase the tax burden on ordinary Nigerians?
Answer_EN: No. 90% of Nigerians and 97% of small businesses will pay the same or less tax.
Keywords: tax burden, ordinary, 90%, small business

Question_EN: How do we know our tax money is being spent well?
Answer_EN: The reforms include transparency laws and a 'Social Contract' where the state must prove value for tax paid.
Keywords: transparency, social contract, accountability

Question_EN: What is the 'Single Agency' collection model?
Answer_EN: The reform aims to have one agency (Nigeria Revenue Service) collect all taxes to stop harassment from multiple agencies.
Keywords: single agency, NRS, collection, harassment

Question_EN: Is it fair that only a few people pay tax in Nigeria?
Answer_EN: No. The reforms aim to broaden the base so everyone pays a small, fair amount rather than a few paying a lot.
Keywords: tax base, fairness, broaden, few taxpayers

Question_EN: How will the new laws handle taxes on social media and crypto?
Answer_EN: A simple, modern framework to tax digital profits and transactions without killing innovation.
Keywords: social media, crypto, digital, framework, innovation

Question_EN: What is being done to stop 'Nuisance Taxes' on traders?
Answer_EN: Over 50 small, annoying taxes and levies are being abolished or merged into one single, easy payment.
Keywords: nuisance tax, traders, abolish, merge, 50

Question_EN: How can I report a tax official who is asking for a bribe?
Answer_EN: Report them to the new 'Tax Ombudsman' office or use the whistleblower framework being set up.
Keywords: bribe, ombudsman, whistleblower, report, official

Question_EN: Can a future government easily change these new fair laws?
Answer_EN: The reforms are being institutionalized via a legal framework to make it harder to return to the old system.
Keywords: reform, legal framework, institutionalized, future

Question_EN: Where can I find more information about these tax reforms?
Answer_EN: Visit fiscalreforms.ng or follow the Presidential Committee on X, LinkedIn, Facebook.
Keywords: fiscalreforms.ng, Presidential Committee, info

Question_EN: Will the 10% VAT increase make food more expensive?

Keywords: VAT, food, 0%, stable, prices

Question_EN: Is the student loan repayment a tax?

Keywords: student loan, debt, not a tax

Question_EN: Is there VAT on 'Danfo' or 'Keke' transport fares?

Keywords: VAT, danfo, keke, transport, exempt

Question_EN: Do I pay VAT on my electricity bill?

Question_EN: What is the 'Tax Amnesty' mentioned by the Committee?

Question_EN: Why does it take more than two weeks to get a Tax Clearance Certificate (TCC)?

Keywords: TCC, 2 weeks, timeline, 3 years, FIRS

Question_EN: Must a full-time housewife or a jobless person pay tax?

Keywords: housewife, jobless, 18+, N800k, threshold

Question_EN: What is 'Self Assessment'?

Question_EN: Do temporary or casual workers need to pay tax?

Keywords: temporary, casual, worker, employee, taxable

Question_EN: How does the new law benefit someone earning N6 million yearly?
Answer_EN: Annual tax drops from N896,000 to N780,000, saving the individual N116,000 in a year.
Keywords: PIT, N6m, savings, N116k, middle income

Question_EN: Can I use Withholding Tax (WHT) to pay my Personal Income Tax?

Keywords: WHT, PIT, offset, credit, final tax

Question_EN: What is the difference between PAYE and Direct Assessment?

Question_EN: Is there an asset limit for small company tax exemptions?

Question_EN: Will the new VAT model change how states get money?

Question_EN: What is the 'Social Contract' in the 2025 Tax Act?

Question_EN: What is the 'Single Agency' collection goal?
Answer_EN: The goal is to have one single agency collect all government revenues to stop harassment by multiple agencies.
Keywords: single agency, NRS, revenues, harassment

Question_EN: Can Nigerian youths work for foreign companies without being double-taxed?

Question_EN: Are student loans considered taxable income?

Question_EN: Is income from foreign property taxable if kept abroad?

Question_EN: Why were tax exemptions for musicians and actors removed?

Question_EN: Do I pay VAT on my monthly household rent?

Question_EN: Is there VAT on solar panels or green energy?

Question_EN: Will bread, milk, and eggs have VAT in 2026?

Question_EN: What is a 'Tax Ombudsman'?

Question_EN: Where can I find official help if I don't understand the new law?

Question_EN: How has 'interest' been redefined and digital assets newly taxed?

Question_EN: What capital gains tax exemptions apply to disposal of shares?

Question_EN: What is the Minimum Effective Tax Rate Provision for MNEs?

Question_EN: What are the filing obligations for individuals under the new Act?

Question_EN: What powers does the Act grant for third-party tax debt collection?

Question_EN: What is the Office of the Tax Ombudsman?

Question_EN: What are the various types of taxes in Nigeria and who are they levied on?

Question_EN: What is a Tax Clearance Certificate (TCC)?

Question_EN: What is a Tax Identification Number (TIN)?

Question_EN: Are foreign companies taxed in Nigeria?

Question_EN: What is tax?
